concepts.name: Synapse
concepts.definition: Junction between two nerve cells where neurotransmitters are released to transmit signals

concepts.name: Axon
concepts.definition: Long projection of a neuron that conducts electrical impulses away from the cell body

concepts.name: Dendrite
concepts.definition: Branched extensions of neurons that receive signals from other neurons

concepts.name: Myelin
concepts.definition: Fatty substance that forms insulating sheaths around nerve fibers to increase signal transmission speed

concepts.name: Serotonin
concepts.definition: Neurotransmitter that regulates mood, sleep, appetite, and other functions

concepts.name: Dopamine
concepts.definition: Neurotransmitter involved in reward, motivation, motor control, and pleasure pathways

concepts.name: GABA
concepts.definition: Gamma-aminobutyric acid, the primary inhibitory neurotransmitter in the central nervous system

concepts.name: Cortex
concepts.definition: Outer layer of the brain responsible for higher-order thinking, sensory processing, and motor control

concepts.name: Hippocampus
concepts.definition: Brain structure critical for learning, memory formation, and spatial navigation

concepts.name: Amygdala
concepts.definition: Brain structure involved in processing emotions, particularly fear and threat detection

concepts.name: Antibody
concepts.definition: Y-shaped proteins produced by B cells that bind to specific antigens to neutralize threats

concepts.name: Antigen
concepts.definition: Substance that triggers an immune response by binding to antibodies or T cell receptors

concepts.name: T Cell
concepts.definition: Type of white blood cell that plays central role in cell-mediated immunity

concepts.name: B Cell
concepts.definition: White blood cell that produces antibodies and provides humoral immunity

concepts.name: Macrophage
concepts.definition: Large phagocytic cells that engulf pathogens, dead cells, and cellular debris

concepts.name: Cytokine
concepts.definition: Signaling proteins that mediate communication between immune cells

concepts.name: Vaccine
concepts.definition: Biological preparation that provides immunity against specific diseases

concepts.name: Inflammation
concepts.definition: Immune response characterized by redness, swelling, heat, and pain to combat infection or injury

concepts.name: Complement System
concepts.definition: Cascade of proteins that enhance immune response and destroy pathogens

concepts.name: Immunoglobulin
concepts.definition: Class of proteins that function as antibodies in immune response

concepts.name: CRISPR
concepts.definition: Gene editing technology that allows precise modification of DNA sequences

concepts.name: Epigenetics
concepts.definition: Study of heritable changes in gene expression that don't involve DNA sequence changes

concepts.name: Transcription
concepts.definition: Process of copying DNA sequence into RNA

concepts.name: Translation
concepts.definition: Process of synthesizing proteins from mRNA templates using ribosomes

concepts.name: Codon
concepts.definition: Three-nucleotide sequence that specifies which amino acid to add during protein synthesis

concepts.name: Intron
concepts.definition: Non-coding DNA sequence that is removed during RNA processing

concepts.name: Exon
concepts.definition: Coding DNA sequence that remains in mature mRNA after splicing

concepts.name: Plasmid
concepts.definition: Small DNA molecule separate from chromosomes, commonly used in genetic engineering

concepts.name: Ribosome
concepts.definition: Cellular structure where protein synthesis occurs

concepts.name: Polymerase
concepts.definition: Enzyme that synthesizes DNA or RNA from nucleotides

concepts.name: Food Chain
concepts.definition: Linear sequence of organisms showing who eats whom in an ecosystem

concepts.name: Biome
concepts.definition: Large ecological area characterized by distinctive plant and animal groups

concepts.name: Symbiosis
concepts.definition: Close ecological relationship between two different species

concepts.name: Parasitism
concepts.definition: Relationship where one organism benefits at the expense of another

concepts.name: Predation
concepts.definition: Interaction where one organism captures and consumes another

concepts.name: Succession
concepts.definition: Predictable sequence of ecological changes in an ecosystem over time

concepts.name: Carrying Capacity
concepts.definition: Maximum population size an environment can sustain indefinitely

concepts.name: Biodiversity
concepts.definition: Variety of life forms within ecosystems, species, and genes

concepts.name: Niche
concepts.definition: Role and position of species within ecosystem including habitat and resource use

concepts.name: Trophic Level
concepts.definition: Position in food chain based on feeding relationships

concepts.name: Biofilm
concepts.definition: Community of microorganisms embedded in protective matrix on surfaces

concepts.name: Prion
concepts.definition: Infectious protein particle that causes neurodegenerative diseases

concepts.name: Archaea
concepts.definition: Domain of single-celled microorganisms distinct from bacteria and eukaryotes

concepts.name: Phage
concepts.definition: Virus that infects and replicates within bacteria

concepts.name: Antibiotic Resistance
concepts.definition: Ability of microorganisms to survive exposure to antibiotics

concepts.name: Quorum Sensing
concepts.definition: Cell-to-cell communication system bacteria use to coordinate group behaviors

concepts.name: Extremophile
concepts.definition: Microorganism that thrives in extreme environmental conditions

concepts.name: Horizontal Gene Transfer
concepts.definition: Movement of genetic material between organisms other than by vertical descent

concepts.name: Pathogenicity
concepts.definition: Ability of microorganism to cause disease in host

concepts.name: Microbiome
concepts.definition: Community of microorganisms living in specific environment like human gut

relations.description: Synapses transmit signals using serotonin as neurotransmitter
Synapses transmit signals using dopamine as neurotransmitter
Synapses transmit inhibitory signals using GABA
Axons terminate at synapses to transmit signals
Dendrites receive signals at synaptic junctions
Myelin sheaths insulate axons to increase conduction speed
Cortical regions connect to hippocampus for memory processing
Hippocampus modulates amygdala emotional responses
Antibodies specifically bind to antigens
B cells produce and secrete antibodies
T cells recognize antigens via TCR receptors
Macrophages present antigens to T cells
Cytokines activate and coordinate T cell responses
Cytokines activate B cell proliferation and differentiation
Vaccines stimulate antibody production for immunity
Inflammatory responses are mediated by cytokines
Complement system enhances antibody effectiveness
Immunoglobulins are the protein class of antibodies
CRISPR can modify genes affecting transcription
Transcription produces mRNA for translation
Ribosomes are the cellular machines that perform translation
Codons specify amino acids during translation
Introns are spliced out leaving exons in mature mRNA
RNA polymerase catalyzes transcription process
Epigenetic modifications regulate transcription
Plasmids can carry CRISPR components for gene editing
Food chains are organized by trophic levels
Predation relationships drive food chain structure
Carrying capacity varies across different biomes
Parasitism is one type of symbiotic relationship
Succession processes shape biome development
Biodiversity levels vary significantly across biomes
Overlapping niches can lead to symbiotic relationships
Biofilm formation is regulated by quorum sensing
Antibiotic resistance genes spread via horizontal transfer
Phages can mediate horizontal gene transfer between bacteria
Many archaea are extremophiles
Microbiomes exhibit symbiotic relationships with hosts
Pathogenicity is often enhanced by biofilm formation
Prions exhibit pathogenicity causing disease
Dopamine modulates cortical function and attention
Serotonin influences hippocampal neuroplasticity
GABA provides inhibitory control of amygdala activity
Macrophages trigger inflammatory responses
Complement activation promotes inflammation
Vaccines activate T cell mediated immunity
CRISPR components are often delivered via plasmids
Host epigenetics can be influenced by microbiome
Plasmids are major mediators of horizontal gene transfer
Microbial diversity is a major component of biodiversity
Ecological succession involves changes in microbial communities
Extremophiles inhabit the most extreme biomes
Phages help shape bacterial microbiome composition
Quorum sensing regulates virulence and pathogenicity
Antibiotic resistance is enhanced within biofilms
Transcription requires RNA polymerase enzyme
Translation machinery reads codons to determine amino acids
Exons contain coding sequences for translation
Predation and parasitism are different feeding strategies
Predation helps regulate populations within carrying capacity
Species niche determines its position in food chain
Biodiversity typically increases during succession
Trophic levels are structured by predation relationships
Cortical white matter contains myelinated axons
Hippocampal neurons form extensive dendritic trees
Amygdala projects to other brain regions via axons
Immunoglobulins are produced by B cells
Complement proteins can activate macrophages
Vaccines induce production of specific immunoglobulins
Archaea participate in horizontal gene transfer
Microbiomes often contain biofilm communities
Many extremophiles are classified as archaea
Prion transmission is independent of horizontal gene transfer
Pathogenic behaviors are often coordinated by quorum sensing
Ribosomes decode codons during protein synthesis
Introns are transcribed by RNA polymerase but later removed
Epigenetic modifications can be targeted by CRISPR systems
